questions.question: What data connectors does NovaLens support?
questions.ground_truth_answer: NovaLens supports connectors for Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Redshift, and PostgreSQL.
questions.raw_answer:
NovaLens ships with the following out-of-the-box connectors (exact names as they appear in the UI):

Relational / MPP  
- Amazon Redshift  
- Google BigQuery  
- Microsoft SQL Server  
- MySQL  
- Oracle  
- PostgreSQL  
- Snowflake  
- Teradata  

Cloud file & object stores  
- Amazon S3  
- Azure Data Lake Storage (Gen 2)  
- Google Cloud Storage  

NoSQL & document  
- MongoDB  
- Cassandra / Astra DB  

Streaming & message bus  
- Apache Kafka (Confluent Cloud & on-prem)  
- Amazon Kinesis  
- Google Pub/Sub  

Application / SaaS  
- Salesforce  
- ServiceNow  
- Workday  
- NetSuite  
- SAP (ECC & S/4 HANA via OData & RFC)  
- Jira (Cloud & Data Center)  
- Coupa  
- Zendesk  
- HubSpot  
- Marketo  
- Google Analytics 4  
- Adobe Analytics  

On-prem file & legacy  
- SFTP / FTPS  
- HDFS  
- IBM i (DB2 for i)  
- AS/400 (via JD Edwards connector)  

BI & semantic layers  
- Tableau (extract & live)  
- Power BI (Premium & Pro)  
- Looker  

All connectors support full or incremental extract, schema-on-read inference, and push-down SQL where the source allows.
questions.final_answer:
NovaLens ships with the following out-of-the-box connectors (exact names as they appear in the UI):

Relational / MPP  
- Amazon Redshift  
- Google BigQuery  
- Microsoft SQL Server  
- MySQL  
- Oracle  
- PostgreSQL  
- Snowflake  
- Teradata  

Cloud file & object stores  
- Amazon S3  
- Azure Data Lake Storage (Gen 2)  
- Google Cloud Storage  

NoSQL & document  
- MongoDB  
- Cassandra / Astra DB  

Streaming & message bus  
- Apache Kafka (Confluent Cloud & on-prem)  
- Amazon Kinesis  
- Google Pub/Sub  

Application / SaaS  
- Salesforce  
- ServiceNow  
- Workday

- SAP (ECC & S/4 HANA via OData & RFC)  
- Jira (Cloud & Data Center)  
- Coupa  
- Zendesk  
- HubSpot  
- Marketo  
- Google Analytics 4  
- Adobe Analytics  

On-prem file & legacy

- HDFS

questions.question: How does NovaAI's performance review cycle work?
questions.ground_truth_answer: NovaAI runs a bi-annual performance review cycle. The mid-year review calibration happens in July with results delivered by July 31. The annual review calibration is in January with results by January 31. Ratings use a 5-point scale (Exceptional, Exceeds Expectations, Meets Expectations, Developing, Unsatisfactory). A rating of Developing or below triggers a PIP within 30 days.
